MDA MISSION
To develop, deploy, and sustain layered and integrated missile defense capabilities as part of a comprehensive missile defeat approach to defend the United States, its deployed forces, allies, and friends from missile attacks in all phases of flight.

Dec. 10, 2024: An Army Navy / Transportable Radar Surveillance and Control Series 6 (AN/TPY-6) positioned on the island of Guam tracks a ballistic missile target during Flight Experiment Mission – 02 (FEM-02). (courtesy photo/released)
Read More
about this story
July 12, 2021: Technicians with the Missile Defense Agency Nanosat Testbed Initiative and VOX Space hold one of two CubeSats MDA successfully deployed to space June 30, 2021, aboard a VOX Space, LLC LauncherOne rocket as part of a payload-sharing arrangement with the Department of Defense’s Space Test Program. (courtesy photo/released)
Read More
about this story
Feb. 8, 2024: A Standard Missile -3 Block IIA, or SM-3 Blk IIA, is launched from U.S. Navy Arleigh Burke-class guided missile destroyer USS McCampbell (DDG 85) off the coast of the Pacific Missile Range Facility, Hawaii, during Flight Test Other-23 or FTX-23. (courtesy photo/released)
Read More
about this story
Dec. 11, 2023: An upgraded Ground Based Interceptor with a Capability Enhanced-II Block 1 Exo-Atmospheric Kill Vehicle (EKV) is launched from Vandenberg Space Force Base, California, during Flight Test Ground-based Midcourse Defense Weapon System-12 (FTG-12). (courtesy photo/released)
Read More
about this story
Mar. 29, 2022: MDA, in partnership with PEO Missiles and Space, SMDC, and BMDED, successfully conduct a flight test of the Terminal High Altitude Area Defense (THAAD) Weapon System. Flight Test THAAD Weapon System (FTT)-21 was conducted at the White Sands Missile Range, New Mexico. (courtesy photo/released)
Read More
about this story
Oct. 15, 2024: The Sea-Based X-Band Radar (SBX) enters Pearl Harbor for in-port maintenance following more than 500 days of operational deployment in support of the homeland defense mission. (courtesy photo/released)
Read More
about this story
Mar. 24, 2025:  A Medium Range Ballistic Missile with a Hypersonic Target Vehicle (HTV) - 1 front end is air launched from a C-17 aircraft off the coast of the Pacific Missile Range Facility, Hawaii, during Flight Test Other-40, or FTX-40.(courtesy photo/released)
Read More
